baseline_regression_note: Real executor-subagent runs in isolated forked workspaces scored below the earlier fixture-only baseline. Keep README and result aligned to the real run, not the weaker prior method.

already-split-structure.notes: Executor subagent rerun passed in an isolated forked workspace. Zero-edit path is clearer after the public-child and scannable clarifications, but some qualitative thresholds remain.

invalid-split-structure.notes: Executor subagent rerun passed in an isolated forked workspace. Validation order is good, but duplicate-entrypoint and hidden-public-child handling still require meaningful judgment.

contradictory-instructions.notes: Executor subagent rerun passed in an isolated forked workspace. New contradiction guidance clearly forced the correct early-stop outcome.

large-file.notes: Executor subagent rerun passed in an isolated forked workspace. Folder-backed topic choice is better bounded, but some rewrite/detail thresholds remain qualitative.

existing-child-targeted-update.notes: Executor subagent rerun passed in an isolated forked workspace. Targeted update behavior is workable, but rename/reuse choices still require more judgment than the baseline allowed.

iteration_1: Added contradiction preflight ahead of structural edits.
Added persisted eval scenarios for no-op, repair, and contradiction-stop behavior.
iteration_2: Clarified that a user-mentioned nearby instruction file or local constraint must be read before refactoring.
Closed the remaining ambiguity in the contradiction-stop scenario.
coverage_extension: Ran the persisted large-file create-mode brief against agentic-markdown and recorded a pass.
Ran the persisted existing-child-targeted-update brief against agentic-markdown and recorded a pass.
iteration_3: Made labeled markdown links under Source canonical while keeping raw-path Source lines valid.
Updated persisted eval briefs and fixture scripts to exercise labeled Source links across the full test set.
iteration_4: Changed the repo rule and skill-autoresearch instructions so tests/evals must use separate executor subagents with isolated workspaces and per-step confusion logs.
Ran real executor-subagent tests for all five persisted briefs and recorded the lower but more credible average.
iteration_4_retries: The first rerun batch hit subagent transport disconnects; retried the missing briefs in smaller batches.
Sandbox blocked true git worktree usage, so executor isolation used forked workspaces instead of literal git worktrees.
